Blog Changelog Blogs and changelogs are page groups with special features — RSS feeds, author cards, date ordering, and sidebar navigation. Here's how to set them up. Blog Setup A blog in sitemd is a group of pages with an RSS feed. You need two things: 1. Define the Blog Group In , add a blog group with for automatic RSS: The setting generates an RSS feed at automatically. 2. Write Blog Posts Each blog post is a markdown file in with frontmatter: Author Cards Add an author card at the end of any blog post. Author cards show an avatar, name, role, bio, and social links: Sarah Chen Head of Product Sarah writes about product strategy, developer tools, and building in public. Twitter GitHub LinkedIn Changelog Setup A changelog is a regular page with version entries. No special configuration needed — just write the markdown: Example Changelog Page Rendered Example Here's what a changelog section looks like when rendered: v2.1.0 — April 2026 Brand icons — 3,400+ brand logos from Simple Icons available with Content alignment — Center or right-align text, buttons, images, and cards with fences Hidden content — Visually hide content for SEO and accessibility with fences v2.0.0 — March 2026 User authentication — Login, signup, magic links, and session management Dynamic data — Connect pages to live data from Supabase, Firebase, or any REST API Forms — Inline YAML forms with webhooks, multi-page flows, and conditional logic Tips Use to let your coding agent generate changelog entries from your git history Use to generate blog post drafts from a topic description RSS feeds work with any group — not just blogs. Add to any group definition For the full reference on blogs, RSS, and content generation, see the sitemd docs .

h.c: Blogs and changelogs are page groups with special features — RSS feeds, author cards, date ordering, and sidebar navigation. Here's how to set them up.
A blog in sitemd is a group of pages with an RSS feed. You need two things:
In settings/groups.md , add a blog group with feed: true for automatic RSS: The feed: true setting generates an RSS feed at /blog/feed.xml automatically.
Each blog post is a markdown file in pages/blog/ with groupMember frontmatter:

Add an author card at the end of any blog post. Author cards show an avatar, name, role, bio, and social links: Sarah Chen Head of Product Sarah writes about product strategy, developer tools, and bui

Client Portal A client portal gives your customers a private, logged-in space to track projects, view invoices, and submit requests. sitemd handles authentication, gated content, dynamic data, and forms — all configured through markdown. Authentication Setup Start by configuring auth so clients can log in. sitemd supports Supabase, Firebase, Clerk, and Auth0. Settings Configure your auth provider in : Then set your credentials via the CLI (secrets never go in markdown files): What You Get Login and signup pages — Pre-built in with magic link or password auth Session management — Automatic token refresh, login persistence, and logout User data — Access , anywhere Client tiers — Gate content by (e.g., standard, premium, enterprise) Project Dashboard Show clients their active projects using auth-filtered dynamic data. Each project opens a detail modal with tabs for overview, timeline, and shared files. Dynamic Data Setup What It Looks Like Here's a project detail modal with tabs — click to explore: Overview Timeline Files Website Redesign Status: In Progress Budget: $12,500 / $18,000 Timeline: Jan 15 — Mar 30, 2026 Lead: Sarah Chen The homepage and product pages are complete. Currently working on blog templates and mobile optimization. Message Team Milestones Jan 15 — Project kickoff ✓ Feb 1 — Homepage wireframes ✓ Feb 15 — Design review ✓ Mar 1 — Development sprint (current) Mar 15 — QA and testing Mar 30 — Launch Shared Documents Brand Guidelines v2.pdf Wireframes — Final.fig Content Inventory.xlsx Analytics Report — Q1.csv Request File Access View Project Details View All Projects Invoice Table Display invoices in a table filtered to the logged-in client. Each row shows the invoice number, date, amount, and payment status. Dynamic Data Setup What It Looks Like Invoice Date Amount Status INV-2026-041 Mar 15, 2026 $4,500 ✅ Paid INV-2026-033 Feb 15, 2026 $4,500 ✅ Paid INV-2026-028 Jan 15, 2026 $6,000 ✅ Paid INV-2026-052 Apr 1, 2026 $3,000 ⏳ Pending INV-2026-019 Dec 15, 2025 $4,500 ✅ Paid Gated Client Tiers Show different content based on the client's subscription tier. Use fences to restrict sections to specific user types. Standard Project dashboard, invoice history, file sharing, and support form. Included for all clients. Premium Everything in Standard, plus real-time project analytics, priority support queue, and dedicated account manager. Upgrade Enterprise Everything in Premium, plus custom integrations, SLA guarantees, and white-label reporting. Contact Sales Support Request Form Let clients submit support requests or change orders directly from the portal. This form posts to any webhook endpoint. Subject * Priority Low Medium High Related Project Message * Submit Request Thanks! This is a demo form — nothing was sent. Portal Structure A complete client portal typically has: Directory Purpose Public marketing pages, login prompt Login, signup, forgot password Client dashboard, project list, invoices Premium-tier analytics and reports Enterprise admin and integrations Project detail views, support forms Auth provider configuration Data source for projects and invoices For the full reference on auth, gating, forms, and dynamic data, see the sitemd docs .

b: ⚠️ This is a fully interactive demo site 👈 click the pointer tab over there (or use or ) for a live markdown editor you can use to demo pretty much anything sitemd can do by editing live markdown. Community Hub A community hub brings your project's roadmap, changelog, integrations, and communication channels together in one place. Here's how to build one with sitemd. Roadmap Section Use headings and lists to create a clear roadmap. This pattern works for any project — open source, product, or internal. Shipped User authentication — Login, signup, magic links, and session management Brand icons — 3,400+ brand logos from Simple Icons with Content alignment — Center or right-align any content block with and fences Mobile sidebar — Auto-close menu, merged hamburger, dynamic scroll In Progress Email templates — Transactional email templates in markdown Multi-language support — Serve translated content from the same project Planned Webhooks dashboard — View and replay form submissions Plugin system — Extend sitemd with community-built plugins The markdown for this is just headings and lists — no special syntax: Integration Cards Show your ecosystem with brand icon cards. Use the prefix with Simple Icons names and for brand colors. GitHub Source code, issues, and pull requests. Star us to stay updated. Discord Join the community for help, feature requests, and showcases. npm Install sitemd from npm. Updated with every release. Reddit Discussions, tutorials, and community showcases on r/sitemd. Video Embeds Embed project walkthroughs, tutorials, or community calls. sitemd auto-detects the provider and makes embeds responsive: Supported providers: YouTube, Vimeo, Spotify, Twitter/X, Reddit, Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, CodePen. Tooltips for Context Add inline context to technical terms without cluttering the page: sitemd uses MCP Model Context Protocol — a standard that lets AI coding agents interact with tools programmatically for agent integration and SSG Static Site Generation — your site is pre-built as HTML files, no server required at runtime for deployment. Contact Form Let your community reach you directly: Name Email * Type Bug Report Feature Request General Feedback Message * Send Feedback Thanks for the feedback! This is a demo form — nothing was sent. Hidden Content for SEO Use fences to add content that search engines and screen readers can see, but visitors don't: sitemd community hub — open source markdown site builder, community resources, roadmap, changelog, integrations, developer tools This is useful for adding SEO keywords, an invisible H1, or accessibility descriptions without affecting the visual design. For the full reference on embeds, forms, tooltips, and icons, see the sitemd docs .

Course Platform A course platform needs a catalog, lesson content gated by subscription tier, instructor profiles, and enrollment flows. sitemd handles this with dynamic data, gated fences, author cards, and forms. Course Catalog Display courses as image cards with instructor, duration, and difficulty level. Each card opens a detail modal with curriculum and instructor bio. Dynamic Data Setup What It Looks Like Full-Stack Web Development Sarah Chen · 12 weeks · Beginner to Intermediate View Course Data Science with Python James Rivera · 10 weeks · Intermediate View Course UI/UX Design Fundamentals Maya Patel · 8 weeks · Beginner View Course Course Detail Modal When a visitor clicks a course, a tabbed modal shows the overview, full curriculum, and instructor profile: Overview Curriculum Instructor Full-Stack Web Development 12 weeks · 48 lessons · Beginner to Intermediate 2,340 students enrolled Go from zero to deploying a full-stack web application. This course covers HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Node.js, and PostgreSQL — with hands-on projects every week. Build 6 real projects from scratch Weekly live Q A sessions with the instructor Access to private Discord community Certificate of completion Enroll Now Preview Free Lessons Course Curriculum Module 1 — Foundations (Free preview) Welcome and setup HTML structure and semantics CSS layout and responsive design JavaScript fundamentals Module 2 — Frontend (Pro) 5. React components and state 6. Routing and navigation 7. Forms and validation 8. API integration Module 3 — Backend (Pro) 9. Node.js and Express 10. Database design with PostgreSQL 11. Authentication and sessions 12. REST API design Module 4 — Deployment (Pro) 13. Testing and CI/CD 14. Cloud deployment 15. Performance optimization 16. Final project Your Instructor Sarah Chen Senior Engineer · 10 years experience Sarah has taught over 5,000 students online and worked at Stripe, Vercel, and GitHub. She specializes in making complex topics approachable for beginners. Twitter GitHub My goal is to get you building real things in the first week — not just watching videos. — Sarah Chen View Course Details Gated Content Tiers The heart of a course platform: free preview lessons for everyone, and premium content gated behind a subscription. Use fences to control access. Free vs Pro Content Page-Level Gating Gate entire lesson pages by placing them in : Tier Cards Free Access Module 1 (4 lessons), community Discord, and weekly newsletter. No credit card required. Pro — $29/mo All lessons, live Q A sessions, project reviews, certificate of completion, and priority support. Upgrade Enterprise Team licenses, custom curriculum, dedicated instructor, analytics dashboard, and SSO integration. Contact Sales Student Dashboard Show enrolled students their courses and progress using auth-filtered dynamic data: A student's dashboard might look like: Course Progress Last Accessed Full-Stack Web Development 24 / 48 lessons (50%) Today Data Science with Python 8 / 40 lessons (20%) 3 days ago UI/UX Design Fundamentals 32 / 32 lessons (100%) ✅ Last week Enrollment Form Let visitors enroll in a course. This form posts to a webhook — connect it to your payment processor, student database, or email automation: Full Name * Email * Course * Experience Level Complete beginner Some coding experience Working professional What do you hope to learn? Start Learning Thanks for enrolling! This is a demo — no enrollment was created. Platform Structure A complete course platform typically has: Directory Purpose Homepage, course catalog, pricing, about Individual course overview pages Student login and signup Student dashboard, progress, settings Pro-tier lesson content Enterprise features Course detail views, enrollment forms Student auth configuration Course and enrollment data sources For the full reference on gated content, dynamic data, and forms, see the sitemd docs .

b: ⚠️ This is a fully interactive demo site 👈 click the pointer tab over there (or use or ) for a live markdown editor you can use to demo pretty much anything sitemd can do by editing live markdown. Documentation Site A docs site needs sidebar navigation, anchor links, code blocks, and search. sitemd handles all of this with markdown files and one settings file. The sidebar you see on this page right now is a working example. What You Need A documentation site in sitemd is three things: Markdown pages in — each file is a doc page A group in — defines the sidebar and page order Frontmatter on each page — connects pages to the group That's it. No framework, no config files, no plugins. Step 1: Create Your Pages Each doc page is a markdown file with a title and group membership: The field puts this page in the group. The field tells sitemd to display the docs sidebar on this page. Headings automatically generate anchor IDs — becomes , and these appear as sub-items in the sidebar. Step 2: Configure the Group Define your docs group in : The field controls where this group appears: — shows as a sidebar on the listed pages — shows as a dropdown in the header navigation Indented items under each page are anchor links — they appear as sub-items in the sidebar and link directly to that section. Step 3: Add Search Search is enabled by default. Users can press (or ) to search across all pages. To customize: Search indexes all page titles, descriptions, headings, and content automatically. Code Blocks Documentation sites need great code blocks. sitemd supports syntax highlighting for any language: Tables Tables work with standard markdown syntax: Setting Default Description Enable site-wide search Show the light/dark/paper toggle How the brand appears in the header Show login/signup buttons Real-World Example The sitemd.cc documentation is built exactly this way — 65 pages with sidebar navigation, anchor links, code blocks, and search. All markdown files. For the full reference on groups, sidebars, and navigation, see the sitemd docs .

Event Site An event site needs event listings, detailed schedules, speaker profiles, and a registration flow. sitemd handles this with cards, tabbed modals, author cards, and forms. Event Listings Display upcoming events as cards with dates, locations, and descriptions. Each card opens a detail modal with the full schedule and speakers. Dynamic Data Setup What It Looks Like Frontend Summit 2026 June 12-13, 2026 · San Francisco, CA · In-person View Event Design Systems Workshop July 8, 2026 · Remote · Online workshop View Event Startup Meetup — Portland August 3, 2026 · Portland, OR · In-person View Event Event Detail Modal Full event details in a tabbed modal — overview, schedule, and speakers: Details Schedule Speakers Frontend Summit 2026 June 12-13, 2026 · Moscone Center, San Francisco, CA $299 Early Bird · Limited to 500 attendees Tickets are selling fast — 340 of 500 claimed Two days of talks, workshops, and networking for frontend engineers. Job Board A job board needs filterable listings, detailed role descriptions, and an application flow. sitemd handles this with dynamic data cards, tabbed detail modals, and forms — all from markdown. Job Listings Display open positions as cards with role, company, location, and type. Each card links to a detail modal with full requirements. Dynamic Data Setup What It Looks Like Senior Frontend Engineer Acme Inc · San Francisco, CA · Full-time View Role Product Designer Basecamp · Remote · Full-time View Role Marketing Manager Stripe · New York, NY · Full-time View Role DevOps Engineer Vercel · Remote · Contract View Role Job Detail Modal When a visitor clicks a listing, a tabbed modal shows the full role description, requirements, and company info: Role Requirements Company Senior Frontend Engineer Acme Inc · San Francisco, CA · Full-time $160K — $200K · Posted 3 days ago We're looking for a senior frontend engineer to lead the redesign of our customer dashboard. You'll work closely with design and product to build fast, accessible interfaces used by millions. Responsibilities: Lead frontend architecture decisions for the dashboard team Build and maintain React components with TypeScript Mentor junior engineers through code reviews and pairing Collaborate with design on prototypes and interaction patterns Improve performance, accessibility, and test coverage Apply Now Save Job Requirements Must have: 5+ years of frontend development experience Strong proficiency in React, TypeScript, and CSS Experience with design systems and component libraries Track record of shipping production applications at scale Excellent communication and collaboration skills Nice to have: Experience with Next.js or Remix Background in data visualization (D3, Chart.js) Contributions to open-source projects Experience mentoring or leading a small team Benefits: Competitive equity package Comprehensive health, dental, and vision Flexible PTO and remote work options $2,500 annual learning and conference budget Home office stipend About Acme Inc Acme Inc Series B · 120 employees · Founded 2019 Acme builds developer tools that help engineering teams ship faster. Used by 10,000+ companies worldwide. Backed by a16z and Sequoia. Website Careers We believe the best tools are built by diverse teams who care deeply about craft. — CEO, Acme Inc View Job Details Filtering by Category Filter listings by department, location, type, or salary range using : Create separate pages for each filter to build targeted landing pages e.g., /jobs/remote-engineering or /jobs/senior-design — each page ranks independently in search engines . Application Form Put the application form inside a modal so candidates can apply without leaving the listing. This form posts to any webhook — connect it to your ATS, email, or Airtable: Apply for This Role Full Name * Email * Portfolio or LinkedIn Years of Experience * Why are you interested in this role? * Submit Application Thanks for applying! This is a demo — no application was sent. Open Application Form Job Listings Table For a denser view, display jobs as a table instead of cards. Good for internal job boards or pages with many listings: Role Department Location Type Salary Senior Frontend Engineer Engineering San Francisco, CA Full-time $160K — $200K Product Designer Design Remote Full-time $130K — $170K Marketing Manager Marketing New York, NY Full-time $110K — $140K DevOps Engineer Engineering Remote Contract $150K — $180K Content Strategist Marketing Chicago, IL Full-time $90K — $120K iOS Engineer Engineering San Francisco, CA Full-time $155K — $195K Board Structure A complete job board typically has: Directory Purpose Homepage, job listings, category pages, about Filtered listing pages (by dept, location, type) Job detail views, application forms Job data source configuration Employer login for posting (optional) For the full reference on dynamic data, forms, and modals, see the sitemd docs .

SaaS Product A SaaS product site needs authentication, gated content, pricing tiers, forms, and dynamic data. sitemd handles all of this as premium features — configured through markdown, no code required. Authentication Add login and signup to your site with one settings file. sitemd supports Supabase, Firebase, Clerk, and Auth0. Setup Configure your auth provider in : Then set your credentials via the CLI (secrets never go in markdown files): What You Get Login and signup pages — Pre-built in with magic link or password auth Session management — Automatic token refresh, login persistence, and logout User data — Access , anywhere on your site Account dashboard — Protected pages in for logged-in users Gated Content Once auth is configured, restrict content by user type. Gate entire pages or inline sections. Inline Gating Wrap any content in a fence to restrict it: Page-Level Gating Place files in with subdirectories matching user types: Pricing Modal Use a tabbed modal for pricing tiers. Visitors click a button to see plans: Starter Pro Enterprise Starter — Free 1 project Community support Basic analytics Get Started Pro — $29/mo Unlimited projects Priority support Advanced analytics Custom domain Upgrade to Pro Enterprise — Custom Everything in Pro SSO and team management Dedicated support SLA guarantee Contact Sales View Pricing Start Free Forms Collect leads, support requests, or feedback with inline forms. Forms submit to any webhook endpoint. Full Name * Work Email * Company Interested In Tell us about your use case Request a Demo Thanks! This is a demo form — nothing was sent. Dynamic Data Connect your pages to live data from Supabase, Firebase, Airtable, or any REST API. Display as cards, tables, lists, or detail views: Auth-Filtered Data Show personalized data to logged-in users: Putting It Together A complete SaaS product site typically has: Directory Purpose Marketing pages, pricing, features Login, signup, forgot password User dashboard, settings, billing Pro-tier features and content Enterprise features and admin Pricing modal, feature tours Auth provider configuration Data source configuration For the full reference on auth, gating, forms, and dynamic data, see the sitemd docs .

What Will You Build? sitemd builds any kind of site from markdown files. Pick a starting point below — each guide shows you the real markdown and settings you need, with live rendered examples on the page. Startup Landing Page Hero sections, feature cards, brand integrations, and call-to-action buttons. Everything a product launch needs. Build this Documentation Site Sidebar navigation, anchor links, code blocks, and search. A full docs site from a handful of markdown files. Build this Blog Changelog Blog posts with author cards and RSS feeds. Changelogs with version entries. All powered by page groups. Build this Portfolio Image galleries, creative layouts, and author bios. Show off your work with visual-first pages. Build this SaaS Product Authentication, gated content, pricing modals, dynamic data, and forms. A full product site with user accounts. Build this Community Hub Roadmaps, changelogs, embeds, contact forms, and brand integrations. Rally your community around your project. Build this Client Portal Authenticated dashboards, project tracking, invoice tables, and gated content tiers. A full client portal with login. Build this Online Store Product catalogs with detail modals, category filtering, image cards, and order forms. An e-commerce storefront from markdown. Build this Real Estate Portfolio Property listings with image carousels, detail modals with specs and galleries, filtering, and inquiry forms. Build this Job Board Job listings with filtering by role, location, and type. Detail modals with requirements and an apply form. Build this Course Platform Course catalogs with gated lesson tiers, instructor profiles, enrollment forms, and student dashboards. Build this Event Site Event listings with schedules, speaker profiles, registration forms, and ticket selection. Build this For the full reference on every feature, visit the sitemd documentation .

b: ⚠️ This is a fully interactive demo site 👈 click the pointer tab over there (or use or ) for a live markdown editor you can use to demo pretty much anything sitemd can do by editing live markdown. Components Everything on this page is built with pure markdown — no HTML, no CSS, no JavaScript. Each section shows a live component followed by the markdown that produces it. Buttons Buttons link to pages, external URLs, or modals. Consecutive button lines form a row. Primary Button Outline Button External Link Big buttons Big Primary Big Outline Colored buttons Red Green Blue Purple Orange Pink You can also use hex colors: Custom Hex Button icons Add an icon to any button with the modifier. Icons come from the Lucide icon set. Download Next Step Learn More Brand Display Render your site's brand name (and logo, if configured) inline anywhere on the page using . Add a width to scale it: . sitemd Demo Site Scaled to 200px wide: {{brandDisplay:200}} Images Basic images work like standard markdown. Add modifiers after the URL for sizing, shapes, filters, and more. Basic image Width and height Shapes Filters Crop and rotation Corner styles Click to expand Click this image to open it in a fullscreen lightbox: Image Rows Image rows display images side by side at equal height. They wrap on mobile. Galleries Galleries create a responsive grid with a built-in lightbox. Click any image to browse. Galleries support modifiers too: Cards Cards arrange content in a responsive grid. Each card can have a title, description, image, and link. Basic cards Getting Started Learn how to create pages, configure settings, and build your first sitemd site. Read the docs Components Every building block available in sitemd — buttons, images, forms, and more. Browse components Blog Sample blog posts showing how to write and organize blog content. Browse components Cards with images Mountain Vista Cards can include images above the title for visual impact. Learn more Ocean Waves Images are responsive and maintain their aspect ratio. Learn more Cards with icons Use to show a Lucide icon instead of an image. Add to tint it. Fast Builds Millisecond rebuilds on every file save. Fully Responsive Every page adapts to any screen size automatically. Open Source No vendor lock-in. Host your site anywhere you want. Brand icons Use the prefix to access 3,400+ brand logos from Simple Icons . Add with the brand's hex color. GitHub Source code and version control. Discord Community chat and support. Figma Design files and prototypes. Vercel Frontend hosting and deployment. Banner cards Add to a card title to make it span the full width, breaking out of the grid. Useful for featured content or section headers. Everything you need to build a site sitemd includes pages, blogs, docs, forms, auth, and more — all from markdown. Documentation Six-page guide covering every aspect of sitemd. Read the docs Components Live examples of every building block. Browse Carousel cards Add multiple lines to a single card to create an image carousel with slide indicators, navigation arrows, and swipe support. Click an image to open the lightbox. Mountain Views Swipe or click the arrows to browse. Click an image to open the lightbox. See more images Ocean Sunset Regular image cards work alongside carousel cards in the same grid. Inline markdown in titles Card titles support inline markdown — bold , italic , and links . New — Carousel Cards Multiple images per card with swipe navigation and lightbox. Read the full docs Six pages covering everything you need to know. Embeds Paste a URL from a supported provider and sitemd handles the rest. Embeds are fully responsive. YouTube Other supported providers sitemd auto-detects and embeds content from these platforms: Provider Example URL YouTube or Vimeo Spotify Twitter / X Reddit Instagram TikTok LinkedIn CodePen Tooltips Hover over highlighted text This is a tooltip. It appears on hover and works inline anywhere. to see a tooltip. Tooltips work in paragraphs Paragraph tooltip , in bold text Bold tooltip , and in links Link tooltip too . Modals Modals are content overlays triggered by links. They can contain any sitemd component. Inline modal This is a modal Modals can contain any markdown content — text, buttons, images, even other components. Got it Open the demo modal Tabbed modal Overview Details Settings This is the first tab. Tabbed modals are great for organizing related content. This is the second tab. Each tab has its own content panel. And a third tab. Switch between them by clicking the tab headers. Open the tabbed modal Global modals Global modals live in the directory and can be triggered from any page. This demo site has one — open it from here . The modal file is . Any file in that directory becomes a global modal with the filename as its ID. Forms Forms collect user input and submit to a webhook endpoint. They support a wide range of field types. Forms are a premium feature — they render as a live, interactiv

b: ⚠️ This is a fully interactive demo site 👈 click the pointer tab over there (or use or ) for a live markdown editor you can use to demo pretty much anything sitemd can do by editing live markdown. Ship modern sites fast with markdown Everything you see here — every page, every component, every setting — is a plain markdown file. No HTML. No CSS. No JavaScript. Just markdown. Example Builds Components List What is sitemd? sitemd is a markdown site builder designed for people who use coding agents like Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex. You describe what you want, your agent writes the markdown, and sitemd turns it into a polished site instantly. No code required. Pages, settings, navigation, themes — everything is configured through markdown files with simple YAML frontmatter. Built for AI workflows. sitemd exposes MCP tools and agent skills that let coding agents create pages, update settings, and deploy your site. Just describe what you want in plain language. Instant feedback. The dev server rebuilds the moment you save a file. No build steps, no waiting, no configuration. What will you build? Startup Landing Page Hero sections, feature cards, brand integrations, and call-to-action buttons. View build example Documentation Site Sidebar navigation, anchor links, code blocks, and search.
